mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market deterioration signals impending economic contraction, causing equity valuations to contract and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and deceleration in retail sales indicates stagflationary pressure, forcing central banks to tighten policy while corporate margins compress, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive returns in greater than 58% of triggered months, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no directional bias with positive frequency below 52% during periods of industrial production growth, or autocorrelation completely dominates the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product confirms sustained aggregate demand expansion, supporting corporate revenue growth and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns during GDP expansion quarters are not significantly higher than baseline with t-test p-value above 0.05, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings above 0.4% trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which increases discount rates and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day returns remain positive in greater than 65% of high-inflation months, or the signal reverses direction when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts indicates synchronized labor and housing market expansion, boosting consumer spending and corporate profitability, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, or positive return frequency drops below 50% when both series expand simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.25%, accommodative monetary policy amplifies the positive equity impact of rising industrial production, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction flips to negative when FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.25%, or 20-day returns during low-rate periods show no statistical edge over random walk benchmarks.
